medications.indication_rationale: The source gave sublingual GTN in person to relax the lower oesophageal sphincter. Recommending GTN remotely is unsafe — it can cause hypotension/syncope (dangerous in someone alone), does not reliably relieve an impaction, and could be given in undifferentiated chest pain. Definitive treatment is endoscopic disimpaction in hospital.
contraindications_in_this_case: Does not resolve an impaction; delays endoscopy

medications.indication_rationale: Source gave oral diazepam in person for smooth-muscle relaxation. A sedating benzodiazepine in a patient who cannot swallow saliva increases aspiration risk and must not be recommended remotely.
contraindications_in_this_case: Sedation increases aspiration risk with pooling secretions

medications.drug_name: effervescent/'fizzy drink' or forced fluids to push the bolus down
medications.indication_rationale: Encouraging the patient to drink or force fluids/food down risks aspiration and worsening obstruction and should not be advised; the patient should stay upright and nil by mouth pending endoscopy.

medications.drug_name: proton pump inhibitor (e.g. esomeprazole) for EoE
medications.indication_rationale: A PPI trial is part of longer-term EoE management but is not an acute treatment for the impaction; it is a decision for after endoscopy/biopsy, not the remote emergency response.

medications.drug_name: topical swallowed corticosteroid (e.g. budesonide/fluticasone) for EoE
medications.indication_rationale: First-line maintenance therapy for confirmed EoE, initiated by gastroenterology after diagnostic endoscopy — not an acute or remote intervention tonight.
These have no role in relieving an acute impaction and should not substitute for urgent endoscopic care; dietary elimination for EoE is a planned gastroenterology/dietetics decision after diagnosis.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: After diagnosis, dietitian-guided elimination diet is a mainstay of EoE management to reduce inflammation and recurrent impactions.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stay sitting upright, do not eat or drink anything (including trying to wash the food down), and spit out saliva into a bowl while arranging emergency transfer.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Upright posture and nil-by-mouth reduce aspiration risk while awaiting endoscopy; forcing fluids can worsen things.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Once the acute event is resolved and EoE confirmed, cut solids small, chew thoroughly, and follow the gastroenterology/dietetics plan to prevent recurrence.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Mechanical and dietary measures reduce future impaction risk in EoE.

patient_education_points.point: When food is stuck and you can't even swallow your own saliva, that's an emergency — you need to go to hospital now, most likely for a camera test (endoscopy) to remove it. It can't be safely fixed at home with medication.

While you arrange to get there: sit upright, don't eat or drink anything or try to wash it down, and spit saliva out rather than swallowing. Call 000 immediately if you have any trouble breathing, severe tearing chest or back pain, or you vomit blood.
Please don't take GTN sprays, sedatives, or fizzy drinks to try to shift it — those can be dangerous when you're on your own and don't reliably clear a stuck bolus.

Your history (asthma/eczema and food catching over months) points to an allergic inflammation of the food pipe called eosinophilic oesophagitis. After the emergency is dealt with, you'll need an endoscopy with biopsies and ongoing treatment to stop this happening again.

follow_up_plan.interval: Emergency now (ED for endoscopic disimpaction); gastroenterology follow-up within days to weeks for endoscopy/biopsy confirmation and EoE management. Call 000 immediately for airway, tearing pain, or haematemesis.
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Successful endoscopic disimpaction, oesophageal mucosal findings and biopsies, ACS assessment (ECG/troponin), and initiation of EoE therapy.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any breathing difficulty or stridor, severe tearing chest/back pain, or vomiting blood — call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognise an ongoing food-bolus impaction with inability to swallow saliva as a time-critical emergency needing urgent ED/endoscopy
Give airway/aspiration precautions (stay upright, nil by mouth, do not force fluids)
Provide explicit emergency safety-netting for airway compromise, perforation, and haematemesis, and consider the cardiac mimic
Arrange gastroenterology follow-up for EoE diagnosis/management after the acute event
acceptable_alternatives: Calling an ambulance is appropriate if there is any airway concern or he cannot get to ED safely alone
PPI/topical steroid/dietary EoE therapy is acceptable only as a post-endoscopy gastroenterology decision
errors_of_omission: Not recognising the impaction as an emergency / advising home management
Not considering ACS as a cause of chest pain
errors_of_commission: Recommending GTN, a benzodiazepine, or effervescent/forced fluids for remote disimpaction
Telling the patient to push the food down with drinking
Delaying ED transfer to trial home measures
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: This is a time-critical emergency: an ongoing oesophageal impaction with inability to swallow saliva requires urgent ED endoscopy, not remote pharmacology. Any home-remedy/GTN/sedative advice or failure to escalate is an automatic safety failure; ACS must also be considered for the chest pain.
